---
title: "Reproducing Deep Learning-Based Aberration Compensation for Fluorescence Microscopy"
author: "Camila Ramirez"
date: "2026-03-03"
categories: [deep learning, microscopy, image processing]
description: "An introduction to my senior project reproducing the DeAbe+ pipeline from Guo et al. 2024"
---

## Overview

For my senior project, I am reproducing the work from the paper 
*"Deep learning-based aberration compensation improves contrast and resolution 
in fluorescence microscopy"* by Min Guo et al. (Nature Communications, 2024).

## What is the paper about?

The paper introduces a deep learning pipeline called **DeAbe+** that improves 
the quality of fluorescence microscopy images. When scientists image biological 
samples like worm embryos, the images get distorted and blurry — especially 
deep inside thick tissue. The authors trained neural networks to fix these 
distortions automatically without needing special hardware.

## The Pipeline

The method works in 3 steps:

1. **De-aberration (Step 1):** Fixes optical distortions using a 3D-RCAN network
2. **Deconvolution (Step 2):** Sharpens the image using another 3D-RCAN network  
3. **Isotropic Enhancement (Step 3):** Improves resolution in all directions using a CARE network

## My Goals

- Download and explore the provided dataset (*C. elegans* worm embryo images)
- Run the pre-trained models on the test data
- Reproduce the key figures from the paper
- Document every step of the process

## Data

The data is publicly available at [Zenodo](https://zenodo.org/records/11277422) 
and was also kindly shared directly by the authors' lab.
